title: Ensure clusters are created with Private Nodes

input_row.title: Ensure clusters are created with Private Nodes
input_row.description: Private Nodes are nodes with no public IP addresses. Disable public IP addresses for cluster nodes, so that they only have private IP addresses.
input_row.rationale: Disabling public IP addresses on cluster nodes restricts access to only internal networks, forcing attackers to obtain local network access before attempting to compromise the underlying Kubernetes hosts.
input_row.audit: Using Google Cloud Console: 1. Go to Kubernetes Engine by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/kubernetes/list. 2. Select the desired cluster, and within the Details pane, make sure Private Clusters is set to Enabled. Using Command Line: To check Private Nodes status for an existing cluster, first define 2 variables Cluster Name and Zone and then run the following command: gcloud container clusters describe $CLUSTER_NAME --zone $COMPUTE_ZONE -- format json | jq '.privateClusterConfig.enablePrivateNodes' The output of the above command returns { "enablePrivateNodes": true } `if Private Nodes is enabled. Internal Only - General
input_row.remediation: Once a cluster is created without enabling Private Nodes, it cannot be remediated. Rather the cluster must be recreated. Using Google Cloud Console: 1. Go to Kubernetes Engine by visiting: https://console.cloud.google.com/kubernetes/list. 2. Click CREATE CLUSTER. 3. Configure the cluster as required then click Networking under CLUSTER in the navigation pane. 4. Under IPv4 network access, click the Private cluster radio button. 5. Configure the other settings as required, and click CREATE. Using Command Line: To create a cluster with Private Nodes enabled, include the --enable-private-nodes flag within the cluster create command: gcloud container clusters create --enable-private-nodes Setting this flag also requires the setting of --enable-ip-alias and --master-ipv4- cidr=.
input_row.impact: To enable Private Nodes, the cluster has to also be configured with a private master IP range and IP Aliasing enabled. Private Nodes do not have outbound access to the public internet. If you want to provide outbound Internet access for your private nodes, you can use Cloud NAT or you can manage your own NAT gateway. To access Google Cloud APIs and services from private nodes, Private Google Access needs to be set on Kubernetes Engine Cluster Subnets.
input_row.default_value: By default, Private Nodes are disabled.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_gke_cluster_private_nodes_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
The automated program would check the following:

- Use the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) command `gcloud container clusters describe` to retrieve the private nodes configuration for GKE clusters.
- Specifically, the command will be:  
  ```
  gcloud container clusters describe <CLUSTER_NAME> --zone <COMPUTE_ZONE> --format json
  ```
- This command will output the cluster configuration in JSON format which we will then check for the property. The relevant JSON path to check is:
  ```json
  .privateClusterConfig.enablePrivateNodes
  ```
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - If the value of `enablePrivateNodes` is `true`, this means Private Nodes are enabled, which is compliant.
  - If the value is `false`, this indicates non-compliance as Private Nodes are not enabled.
- The program will iterate over all clusters in the specified project/region and output results through a compliance report showing which clusters are compliant or not based on the Private Nodes configuration.
- Ensure to handle potential errors in cases where the cluster does not exist or other API-related issues may arise.

This automation effectively assesses compliance based on the configuration of GKE clusters in relation to private nodes, as detailed in the CIS GKE Benchmark.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The use of the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) command to retrieve and check the configuration of GKE clusters for private nodes is an appropriate automated method.
- This can indeed be checked via GCP APIs/Google Cloud SDK (gcloud)/CLI. The `gcloud container clusters describe` command is a valid and efficient way to obtain the necessary configuration details.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 9

- The automation details are technically accurate. The use of the `gcloud container clusters describe` command with the specified JSON path `.privateClusterConfig.enablePrivateNodes` is correct for determining if private nodes are enabled.
- The command syntax is valid, and the method for checking compliance is sound.
- The program name follows a reasonable convention but could be slightly more descriptive.
- The handling of potential errors is mentioned, which is good practice, though specifics on error handling are not detailed.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- The program name could be improved for clarity. A suggestion would be `gcp_gke_cluster_private_nodes_compliance_check` to better reflect the compliance aspect.
- Consider adding more detailed error handling and logging mechanisms to capture and report API-related issues or non-existent clusters.
- It might be beneficial to include a check for whether the cluster is a private cluster overall, as enabling private nodes is typically part of a broader private cluster configuration.
- An alternative method could involve using the GCP API directly via REST calls, though the `gcloud` command is sufficient for this purpose.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- I have high confidence in this assessment due to the correctness of the technical details and the appropriate use of GCP SDK capabilities.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**

- Modify: Update the program name to `gcp_gke_cluster_private_nodes_compliance_check` for better clarity and descriptiveness.
- Ensure detailed error handling and logging are implemented to handle any API-related issues effectively.
